Adam_p4_i0.description: A schematic diagram of a bent waveguide or channel (yellow) connecting an input source on the left to an output on the right, with a localized probe or interaction region (blue) positioned at the center, likely representing near-field measurement or coupling within the structure.
key_findings: Illustrates signal propagation through a confined, curved waveguide structure
Highlights a central interaction or measurement point where near-field effects are probed
suggested_knowledge_points: Role of probes in detecting localized electromagnetic fields

Safian_p14_i0.description: Schematic of a continuous-wave (CW) terahertz photomixing system where two optical sources at frequencies ω1 and ω2 are combined and directed onto photomixers, generating a THz signal at the difference frequency (ω1−ω2), which is transmitted and received by antenna-coupled devices.
key_findings: THz radiation is generated via photomixing of two optical frequencies, producing a signal at the difference frequency ω1−ω2.
Both transmitter and receiver use photomixers and antennas to emit and detect coherent CW THz radiation.
suggested_knowledge_points: Principle of generating THz waves using two laser frequencies
Role of photoconductive antennas in THz emission and detection
Coherent detection of CW THz signals using optical mixing
